events.text: Sarah Robinson, 72-year-old Black female, Current smoker (0.5 ppd). Lives independently. ECOG performance status 1.
Diagnosed with Pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma of the Head of pancreas. Clinical staging: T2N1M0, AJCC Stage IIB. ICD-10: C25.9. Stage IIB pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma, borderline resectable.
Pathology specimen received for initial biopsy.
PET/CT performed for baseline staging.
Clinical visit for initial oncology consultation.
NGS report received for molecular profiling.

Initiated neoadjuvant chemotherapy: mFOLFIRINOX (Oxaliplatin 85 mg/m2 + Irinotecan 150 mg/m2 + Leucovorin 400 mg/m2 + 5-FU 2400 mg/m2 46h infusion) q2w x 8 cycles. Cycle 1. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.

Initiated neoadjuvant chemotherapy: mFOLFIRINOX (Oxaliplatin 85 mg/m2 + Irinotecan 150 mg/m2 + Leucovorin 400 mg/m2 + 5-FU 2400 mg/m2 46h infusion) q2w x 8 cycles. Cycle 2. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 2 assessment.

Initiated neoadjuvant chemotherapy: mFOLFIRINOX (Oxaliplatin 85 mg/m2 + Irinotecan 150 mg/m2 + Leucovorin 400 mg/m2 + 5-FU 2400 mg/m2 46h infusion) q2w x 8 cycles. Cycle 3. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.

Initiated neoadjuvant chemotherapy: mFOLFIRINOX (Oxaliplatin 85 mg/m2 + Irinotecan 150 mg/m2 + Leucovorin 400 mg/m2 + 5-FU 2400 mg/m2 46h infusion) q2w x 8 cycles. Cycle 4. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 4 assessment.

Initiated neoadjuvant chemotherapy: mFOLFIRINOX (Oxaliplatin 85 mg/m2 + Irinotecan 150 mg/m2 + Leucovorin 400 mg/m2 + 5-FU 2400 mg/m2 46h infusion) q2w x 8 cycles. Cycle 5. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Developed grade 2 nausea. Managed with ondansetron 8 mg q8h PRN and dexamethasone 4 mg daily.

Initiated neoadjuvant chemotherapy: mFOLFIRINOX (Oxaliplatin 85 mg/m2 + Irinotecan 150 mg/m2 + Leucovorin 400 mg/m2 + 5-FU 2400 mg/m2 46h infusion) q2w x 8 cycles. Cycle 6. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 6 assessment.
MRI abdomen with contrast performed for restaging after neoadjuvant chemotherapy.
Clinical visit for results review after neoadjuvant chemotherapy.

Underwent surgical resection: Pancreaticoduodenectomy (Whipple procedure) with portal vein resection and reconstruction. Estimated blood loss 150 mL. Length of stay 3 days. Uncomplicated postoperative course.
Pathology specimen received for surgical pathology.
Clinical visit for postoperative follow-up.
Initiated adjuvant chemotherapy: Gemcitabine 1000 mg/m2 days 1, 8, 15 q4w x 4 cycles. Cycle 1. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Developed grade 2 fatigue. Activity modification recommended. TSH and CBC checked - within normal limits.
Initiated adjuvant chemotherapy: Gemcitabine 1000 mg/m2 days 1, 8, 15 q4w x 4 cycles. Cycle 2. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 2 assessment.
Developed grade 2 fatigue. Activity modification recommended. TSH and CBC checked - within normal limits.
Initiated adjuvant chemotherapy: Gemcitabine 1000 mg/m2 days 1, 8, 15 q4w x 4 cycles. Cycle 3. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Initiated adjuvant chemotherapy: Gemcitabine 1000 mg/m2 days 1, 8, 15 q4w x 4 cycles. Cycle 4. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 4 assessment.
PET/CT performed for restaging after adjuvant chemotherapy.
Clinical visit for results review after adjuvant chemotherapy.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
MRI abdomen with contrast performed for surveillance imaging.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
MRI abdomen with contrast performed for surveillance imaging.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
MRI abdomen with contrast performed for surveillance imaging.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.

documents.text:
PATHOLOGY REPORT

Patient: Sarah Robinson
MRN: patient_cc7feac946ee
Specimen ID: S2023-55031
Date of Procedure: 2023-07-06
Date of Report: 2023-07-09

SPECIMEN: Head of pancreas, excision/biopsy

FINAL DIAGNOSIS:
Pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma, well differentiated.
Greatest tumor dimension: 2.2 cm
Margins: Negative (closest margin 1.2 cm)
Lymphovascular invasion: Not identified
Perineural invasion: Not identified

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY:
CK7: Positive
CK20: Negative
MUC1: Positive
CDX2: Negative
SMAD4: Lost (absent staining)

GROSS DESCRIPTION:
Received fresh labeled "Sarah Robinson - Head of pancreas" is a tan-white tissue fragment measuring 2.2 x 1.5 x 1.1 cm. The cut surface reveals a friable gray-white mass. Specimen entirely submitted in cassettes A1-A8.

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Sections show a well differentiated neoplasm composed of sheets of atypical cells with scant cytoplasm and prominent nucleoli. Mitotic figures are frequent (12 per 10 HPF). Focal necrosis is present. The surrounding stroma shows minimal reactive changes.

Electronically signed by: Michael Torres, MD
Department of Pathology

PET/CT REPORT

Patient: Sarah Robinson
MRN: patient_cc7feac946ee
Date of Study: 2023-07-07

CLINICAL INDICATION: Pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma of the head of pancreas. baseline staging.

COMPARISON: None available.

TECHNIQUE: Multidetector CT performed from skull base to iliac crest following administration of 100 mL Omnipaque 350 IV contrast.

FINDINGS:
LUNGS: Clear. No pulmonary nodules or masses. No pleural effusion.

MEDIASTINUM: No significant lymphadenopathy. Heart size normal.

LIVER: No hepatic metastases identified.

PANCREAS: Hypodense mass in the head of the pancreas measuring 4.3 x 3.0 cm with abutment of the SMA. Pancreatic duct dilated to 6 mm.

KIDNEYS: Normal in size bilaterally. No hydronephrosis or renal masses.

PELVIS: No pelvic mass or lymphadenopathy. Bladder unremarkable.

BONES: No suspicious osseous lesions. Degenerative changes of the lumbar spine.

IMPRESSION:
1. Pancreatic head mass with vascular involvement. Borderline resectable.

Electronically signed by: Robert Chang, MD
Department of Radiology

INITIAL CONSULTATION - Hepatobiliary Surgery

Patient: Sarah Robinson
MRN: patient_cc7feac946ee
Date: 2023-07-15
Provider: Dr. Carlos Mendez

CHIEF COMPLAINT: initial oncology consultation

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:
Mr./Ms. Robinson is a 72-year-old female who presents for initial evaluation of newly diagnosed pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma of the head of pancreas, IIB. Diagnosis made following evaluation of progressive symptoms. Stage IIB pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma, borderline resectable. Pathology confirmed pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma. Molecular testing has been sent. Patient reports fatigue and unintentional 10-lb weight loss over 3 months.

PAST MEDICAL HISTORY:
- obstructive sleep apnea

SURGICAL HISTORY:
- Cesarean section x 2 (if applicable)

SOCIAL HISTORY:
- Current smoker (1 ppd)
- Alcohol: Social drinker (2-3 drinks/week)
- Occupation: Construction worker (retired)
- Lives with spouse and adult children

FAMILY HISTORY:
- No significant family history.

ALLERGIES: Penicillin (rash)

MEDICATIONS:
- None

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS:
Positive for: joint pain, mild dyspnea on exertion, dry mouth, mild cough. Remainder of 14-point ROS reviewed and negative.

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION:
Vitals: BP 148/71, HR 80, RR 18, Temp 97.2F, SpO2 97% on RA, Wt 57.2 kg
General: Alert, oriented, well-appearing, in no acute distress.
HEENT: Normocephalic, atraumatic. Mucous membranes moist.
Neck: No thyromegaly. No lymphadenopathy.
Lungs: Diminished breath sounds at right base. No wheezes.
CV: RRR, S1/S2 normal. No murmurs, gallops, or rubs.
Abdomen: Soft, mildly tender in RUQ. No rebound. Normoactive bowel sounds.
Extremities: No peripheral edema. Good pulses bilaterally.
Neuro: Alert and oriented. No focal neurological deficits.

LABORATORY RESULTS:
WBC 5.1, Hgb 11.6, Plt 201, Cr 0.74

IMAGING:
See separate imaging report.

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
72-year-old female with IIB pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma of the head of pancreas. initial oncology consultation.

1. Pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma of the head of pancreas, Stage IIB
   - Reviewed pathology and imaging with patient and family
   - Recommend neoadjuvant chemotherapy
   - Will discuss at multidisciplinary tumor board
   - Molecular profiling results pending
2. Supportive care
   - Nutrition consultation placed
   - Social work referral for psychosocial support
   - PICC line to be placed prior to treatment
3. Follow-up in 1-2 weeks to initiate treatment

Electronically signed by: Amanda Foster, MD
Hepatobiliary Surgery

NEXT GENERATION SEQUENCING REPORT

Patient: Sarah Robinson
MRN: patient_cc7feac946ee
Date of Report: 2023-07-16
Specimen: Head of pancreas (Core needle biopsy)
Diagnosis: Pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma
Panel: FoundationOne CDx (468 genes)

RESULTS SUMMARY:
Tumor Mutational Burden (TMB): 1.8 mutations/Mb (Low)
Microsatellite Status: MSS

CLINICALLY SIGNIFICANT GENOMIC ALTERATIONS:
  KRAS p.G12D (c.35G>A) - Missense (Exon 2) [Clinically Significant]

ADDITIONAL PATHOGENIC ALTERATIONS:
  CDKN2A Loss - Homozygous deletion

VARIANTS OF UNCERTAIN SIGNIFICANCE:
  BRCA2 p.T3085A - VUS
  PALB2 p.L1009fs - VUS

COPY NUMBER ALTERATIONS:
  CDKN2A homozygous deletion

FUSIONS:
  No pathogenic fusions detected.

THERAPY ASSOCIATIONS:
  - KRAS G12D: Investigational KRAS G12D inhibitors in clinical trials.
  - BRCA/PALB2 VUS noted - genetic counseling recommended.

Note: This test was performed using hybrid capture-based next generation sequencing.
Sequencing depth: mean 684x coverage.
Tumor content: 34%.

Electronically signed by: Katherine Liu, MD, PhD
Molecular Pathology Laboratory

FOLLOW-UP NOTE - Hepatobiliary Surgery

Patient: Sarah Robinson
MRN: patient_cc7feac946ee
Date: 2023-08-15
Provider: Dr. Priya Sharma

CHIEF COMPLAINT: cycle 2 assessment

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:
Mr./Ms. Robinson is a 72-year-old female with IIB pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma of the head of pancreas who presents for cycle 2 assessment. Patient reports mild fatigue but otherwise doing well. Reports mild nausea but eating adequately.

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS:
Positive for: mild dyspnea on exertion, intermittent nausea, decreased appetite, night sweats. Remainder of 14-point ROS reviewed and negative.

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION:
Vitals: BP 132/85, HR 71, RR 15, Temp 98.1F, SpO2 99% on RA, Wt 93.5 kg
General: Comfortable, thin-appearing, no acute distress.
HEENT: No scleral icterus. Oropharynx clear.
Neck: No thyromegaly. No lymphadenopathy.
Lungs: Clear to auscultation bilaterally.
CV: RRR, S1/S2 normal. No murmurs, gallops, or rubs.
Abdomen: Soft, non-distended. Well-healed midline surgical scar.
Extremities: No edema. No cyanosis or clubbing.
Neuro: A&O x3, CN II-XII intact, no focal deficits.

LABORATORY RESULTS:
WBC 4.2, Hgb 11.0, Plt 114, Cr 1.16

IMAGING:
Most recent imaging reviewed.

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
72-year-old female with IIB pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma of the head of pancreas. cycle 2 assessment.

1. Pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma - cycle 2 assessment
   - Treatment well tolerated, continue
   - Will check labs prior to next cycle
2. Symptom management
   - Neuropathy being monitored
3. Follow-up in 6 weeks

Electronically signed by: Amanda Foster, MD
Hepatobiliary Surgery

PATHOLOGY REPORT

Patient: Sarah Robinson
MRN: patient_cc7feac946ee
Specimen ID: S2023-39048
Date of Procedure: 2023-11-26
Date of Report: 2023-11-28

SPECIMEN: Head of pancreas, excision/biopsy

FINAL DIAGNOSIS:
Pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma, moderately differentiated.
Greatest tumor dimension: 1.8 cm
Margins: Positive at inked margin
Lymphovascular invasion: Present
Perineural invasion: Not identified

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY:
CK7: Positive
CK20: Negative
MUC1: Positive
CDX2: Negative
SMAD4: Lost (absent staining)

GROSS DESCRIPTION:
Received fresh labeled "Sarah Robinson - Head of pancreas" is a gray-white tissue fragment measuring 1.8 x 1.3 x 0.9 cm. The cut surface reveals a friable tan mass. Specimen entirely submitted in cassettes A1-A8.

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Sections show a moderately differentiated neoplasm composed of glands of atypical cells with moderate cytoplasm and high nuclear-to-cytoplasmic ratio. Mitotic figures are occasionally seen (3 per 10 HPF). Necrosis is present. The surrounding stroma shows minimal reactive changes.

Electronically signed by: Patricia Williams, MD
Department of Pathology
